section.title: Organizational policy
section.intro: Policy templates, governance frameworks, and readiness assessments for organizations adopting AI tools.

resources.title: AI Policy Template for Nonprofits
resources.author: Community IT
resources.description: Customizable template with data classification framework. Distinguishes between data that can enter AI tools and data that cannot. Designed for organizations without dedicated IT policy staff.

resources.title: AI Readiness for Nonprofits
resources.author: NTEN
resources.description: Framework for assessing organizational readiness across culture, capacity, and governance.

resources.title: Fast Forward AI Policy Builder
resources.author: Fast Forward
resources.description: Step-by-step questionnaire that produces a customized draft AI policy covering data classification, approved tools, training requirements, and governance structure.

resources.title: The Emerging Agentic Enterprise
resources.author: MIT Sloan Management Review
resources.description: Identifies four tensions of agentic AI adoption: delegation vs. control, speed vs. oversight, capability vs. trust, individual vs. institutional.

resources.title: Overcoming Organizational Barriers to AI Adoption
resources.author: Harvard Business Review
resources.description: Employee-centric AI adoption is 7x more effective than top-down mandates. The barrier is whether employees have input into how tools are chosen and deployed.

resources.title: WHO: Ethics and Governance of AI for Health
resources.author: World Health Organization
resources.description: WHO's governance principles for AI in health. Useful for connecting AI policy to existing ethical commitments.

resources.title: HealthAI: AI Governance Resources for Health Organizations
resources.author: HealthAI
resources.description: Governance resources for health-sector organizations. Covers concerns specific to organizations handling health data and working with vulnerable populations. See also Section 7.

resources.title: Ethically Aligned Design: A Vision for Prioritizing Human Well-Being
resources.author: IEEE
resources.description: Framework for designing autonomous and intelligent systems that prioritize human well-being. Rooted in engineering ethics.

resources.title: Stanford HAI: AI Governance Resources
resources.author: Stanford Human-Centered AI Institute
resources.description: Research-backed governance frameworks. More academic than the nonprofit templates.

resources.title: Nonprofit AI Guide
resources.author: Whole Whale
resources.description: Covers tool selection, training approaches, and policy development for nonprofits.

resources.title: Value Sensitive Design: Shaping Technology with Moral Imagination
resources.author: Batya Friedman
resources.description: Framework for designing technology that accounts for human values from the start. The values embedded in tool design shape what tools make easy and what they make invisible.

resources.title: Harvard: Guidelines for Generative AI
resources.author: Harvard University
resources.description: Conservative institutional AI policy. Instructive for seeing where a major institution drew its lines.

resources.title: Considerations for Developing an AI Policy
resources.author: AICPA
resources.description: From the accounting profession. The framework for risk assessment, data classification, and vendor evaluation applies broadly.
